title: ceterach hispanicum
intro: Ceterach hispanicum, also known as the Spanish ceterach or prick-madam, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to soothe and clear the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Ceterach hispanicum can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory health issues.
